skill.name: marketing-psychology
skill.description: Apply behavioral science and mental models to marketing decisions, prioritized using a psychological leverage and feasibility scoring system.
skill.summary: Apply behavioral science and mental models to marketing with ethical, prioritized recommendations.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings were false positives. The backtick patterns are markdown code blocks containing formulas, not shell execution. No cryptographic functions or system reconnaissance code exists. This is a documentation-only skill with no executable code or security risks.

content.value_statement: Marketing teams struggle to apply behavioral science effectively without overwhelming users. This skill selects and scores the most impactful psychological models for your specific situation.
actual_capabilities: Score psychological models using the PLFS (Psychological Leverage & Feasibility Score) framework
Select up to 5 high-impact mental models based on user journey stage
Provide ethical guardrails for each recommendation
Map psychological principles to specific marketing surfaces
Generate testable hypotheses for A/B testing
limitations: Does not execute marketing campaigns or integrate with analytics tools
Requires user input about target behavior and context
Recommendations are advisory and require human validation
Does not replace professional marketing expertise

use_cases.title: Pricing Page Optimization
use_cases.description: Reduce decision paralysis by applying the Paradox of Choice and Anchoring principles to pricing tier presentation.

use_cases.title: Email Campaign Design
use_cases.description: Apply scarcity, social proof, and loss aversion ethically to improve email engagement rates.
use_cases.target_user: Email marketing managers designing campaign sequences

use_cases.title: Landing Page Persuasion
use_cases.description: Use authority bias, framing effects, and default options to guide visitor decision-making.

prompt_templates.title: Basic: Identify relevant mental models
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to improve conversion on my pricing page. Users seem overwhelmed by options. What psychological models should I consider?

prompt_templates.title: Intermediate: Score and prioritize models
prompt_templates.prompt: Score the top psychological models for reducing cart abandonment using PLFS. Focus on the decision stage of the buyer journey.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced: Full implementation plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the top 3 PLFS-scored models to my checkout flow. For each: explain the psychology, specify where to apply it, define test metrics, and include ethical considerations.

prompt_templates.title: Expert: Journey-based strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a psychology-driven strategy across the full customer journey (awareness to retention). Score models for each stage and identify the highest-leverage interventions.

output_examples.input: How can I reduce choice overload on my pricing page?
output: Why it works: Too many options increase cognitive load and decision avoidance
Implementation: Reduce tiers to 3, highlight recommended option, collapse advanced features
Test: Compare 3-tier vs 5-tier presentation on conversion rate
Ethical note: Do not hide material pricing information

output_examples.input: What psychology can improve trial-to-paid conversion?
output: Why it works: Users value what they already possess more than equivalent new items
Implementation: Let users customize during trial, show accumulated usage data at conversion point
Test: Personalized onboarding vs generic onboarding trial flows

best_practices: Always score models using PLFS before recommending
Never recommend more than 5 models per request
Include ethical guardrails with every psychological intervention
Map each model to a specific, testable behavior change
anti_patterns: Recommending all 100+ cognitive biases without prioritization
Using dark patterns like fake scarcity or hidden defaults
Applying models without understanding the target behavior
Ignoring ethical implications of persuasion tactics

faq.question: What is PLFS and how is it calculated?
faq.answer: PLFS (Psychological Leverage & Feasibility Score) rates models 1-5 on Behavioral Leverage, Context Fit, Speed to Signal, and Ethics, then subtracts Implementation Cost. Scores range from -5 to +15.

faq.question: How many mental models should I apply at once?
faq.answer: This skill recommends a maximum of 5 models per scenario. Applying more dilutes focus and makes testing difficult. Start with the highest PLFS-scoring model.

faq.question: Is using psychological principles ethical?
faq.answer: Ethics depends on intent and transparency. This skill includes an Ethics dimension in PLFS scoring and requires guardrails. It explicitly prohibits dark patterns, manipulation, and exploiting vulnerable users.

faq.question: Can this replace A/B testing?
faq.answer: No. Psychological recommendations generate hypotheses to test, not guaranteed outcomes. Always validate with A/B testing before full rollout.

faq.question: Which models work best for awareness-stage marketing?
faq.answer: Awareness stage responds well to Mere Exposure, Availability Heuristic, Authority Bias, and Social Proof. These build familiarity and trust before evaluation begins.

faq.question: What if my PLFS score is low or negative?
faq.answer: Models scoring 0 or below should not be recommended. A low score indicates poor fit, high implementation cost, or ethical concerns. Find alternative approaches.
